title: elaeocarpus leratii
intro: Elaeocarpus leratii, also known as the 'Fragrant Serpent Wood' or 'Wild Pear', is a species of flowering plant in the family Elaeocarpaceae. It is native to New Caledonia and has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant effects. The plant has been found to have several health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving pain, and treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma. The herbal preparations of Elaeocarpus leratii include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be consumed orally to harness its medicinal properties.
